message:
test(ft-conformance): numpy subprocess conformance for torch.linalg.det

Add the first slice of frankentorch-c36b: a numpy.linalg.det
subprocess differential test pinning the precision contract of
FrankenTorch's tensor_linalg_det. Companion to the digamma
conformance harness landed earlier today (8dac193), but for the
linalg surface (square-matrix functions over the LU factorization).

Why
---
FEATURE_PARITY.md claims parity_green for det / slogdet / inv /
solve / eigh / lstsq / svd / qr / cholesky / pinverse / matrix_norm,
but none of them have subprocess conformance against numpy.linalg
or scipy.linalg. The existing in-crate tests only assert single
known values within a loose tolerance, which is enough to catch a
catastrophic regression but misses subtle precision drift.

This commit closes the det slice. The other slices follow the
same pattern.

What
----
torch_linalg_det_numpy_subprocess_conformance:

1. Skip cleanly if python3/numpy isn't on the runner.
2. Drive numpy.linalg.det via Python subprocess that emits each
   result as raw f64 bits in JSON, so the round-trip preserves
   precision.
3. Cover representative matrix shapes:
   - Identity 3×3 and 5×5 (det = 1)
   - Scaled identity (det = α^n)
   - Diagonal matrices (det = product of diagonal)
   - 2×2 / 3×3 / 4×4 random well-conditioned matrices
   - Singular matrices (det = 0)
   - Near-singular Hilbert matrices (det near machine precision)
   - Negative-determinant cases (sign matters)
4. Compare against numpy with REL_TOL = 1e-9, scaled by
   max(|a|, |b|, 1.0) so absolute zero is handled cleanly. The
   scale floor of 1.0 means singular cases (det≈0) compare
   absolutely rather than relatively, which is the right behavior
   when the true answer is exactly 0.
5. NaN==NaN is true so cases that legitimately return NaN compare
   stable.

.beads: file frankentorch-c36b (status=open, priority=2) tracking
the umbrella linalg conformance work; the description enumerates
the full target set so future agents can pick off slices in any
order.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -59,6 +59,7 @@
 {"id":"frankentorch-b5of","title":"torch.special functions lack subprocess conformance: digamma, polygamma, logit, xlog1py, entr, multigammaln","description":"All torch.special.* functions in ft-api/lib.rs are hand-rolled approximations (digamma_approx, polygamma_approx, etc.) but lack subprocess conformance against scipy.special. The existing unit tests assert single known values within 1e-6 — too loose to catch silent precision regressions. Add subprocess conformance harnesses comparing each against scipy.special.{digamma, polygamma, logit, xlog1py, entr, multigammaln}. Pattern: matches the existing libm subprocess conformance tests (torch_erf_erfc_libm_subprocess_conformance etc.) — ctypes-from-libm or scipy-via-numpy oracle, ULP-tolerant comparison.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-29T20:07:12.196533219Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-29T20:07:12.196533219Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-b6n","title":"Audit ft-data loading path for latent correctness bugs","description":"## Context
The explicit parity backlog is empty, but we still need fresh-eyes subsystem audits to find latent correctness bugs hidden behind current green status. Start with ft-data and trace its batching/shuffle/drop_last behavior through tests and adjacent crate usage.

## Goal
Identify and fix any obvious correctness bug in the ft-data loading path, then verify with focused tests and workspace gates.

## Acceptance
- trace ft-data end-to-end
- fix at least one high-confidence correctness bug if found
- add/adjust tests proving the fix
- verify with rch cargo check/clippy/tests","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-04T01:10:45.588213207Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-04T01:20:40.140473566Z","closed_at":"2026-04-04T01:20:40.140223743Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-b8f","title":"ft-api: fail closed on out-of-range indexed write ops","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:40:21.732572151Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-06T21:43:18.086308785Z","closed_at":"2026-04-06T21:43:18.085991304Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"frankentorch-c36b","title":"Linear algebra ops lack subprocess conformance: svd, qr, cholesky, det, slogdet, inv, pinverse, solve, eigh, lstsq, matrix_norm","description":"FEATURE_PARITY.md claims parity_green for these ops but none have subprocess conformance against numpy.linalg / scipy.linalg. Add diff-tests across well-conditioned and edge-case matrices (identity, scaled identity, singular, near-singular, complex eigenstructure). Pattern: matches torch_vector_norm_numpy_subprocess_conformance / torch_diff_numpy_subprocess_conformance — numpy oracle, ULP- or relative-tolerant comparison. Coverage: at least det / slogdet / inv / solve as the highest-impact subset; the others can follow incrementally.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-29T20:20:37.714643912Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-29T20:20:37.714643912Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-c3d","title":"Add CUDA/GPU execution backend","description":"FrankenTorch currently exposes `Device::Cuda` tags and device-guard validation, but all real execution remains CPU-only. There are no CUDA kernels, no device memory model, no dispatcher backend selection beyond CPU, and no runtime integration for GPU execution.

Scope for eventual closure:
1. Add concrete GPU storage/device abstractions beyond enum tagging
2. Implement CUDA dispatch keys and backend routing
3. Port core kernels (elementwise, reductions, matmul, indexing) to GPU
4. Preserve deterministic autograd/DAC evidence across cross-device execution
5. Add conformance and benchmark coverage for CPU↔GPU parity

This bead exists so FEATURE_PARITY.md can truthfully mark CUDA/GPU as deferred rather than silently omitted.","status":"deferred","priority":4,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-07T04:23:50.059185557Z","created_by":"CopperStork","updated_at":"2026-04-28T03:33:49.271814166Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-c4tx","title":"masked_scatter all-false fast path drops source zero-gradient","description":"Reviewer finding in crates/ft-api/src/lib.rs. The current tensor_masked_scatter implementation returns Ok(input) immediately when n_true == 0 at lines 11682-11688. That preserves input passthrough but removes the source tensor from the backward graph entirely. The adjacent regression test masked_scatter_all_false_mask_passes_input_through_with_grad at lines 33742-33761 says source gradient should be zero, but it only asserts input_grad, so the missing source gradient is not covered. Repro by code inspection: create input requires_grad=true, all-zero mask, source requires_grad=true, call tensor_masked_scatter, sum the output, run tensor_backward, then query tensor_gradient(report, source). Because the fast path returns the original input node, compute_reachable in ft-autograd only reaches input and tensor_gradient(source) is None instead of a zero vector of source.numel(). This silently violates the deterministic autograd contract for a tracked source argument. Fix by composing the all-false case through an autograd-aware zero dependency on source, or by adding an explicit masked_scatter op/backward rule that records source and emits zero source gradients when no mask entries consume it. Add a regression assertion that all-false masks produce source_grad == vec![0.0; source_numel].","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-29T18:00:50.510404660Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-29T18:34:57.725416487Z","closed_at":"2026-04-29T18:34:57.723645605Z","close_reason":"Removed the n_true==0 fast-path return-input shortcut for non-empty source — the gather + where composition now runs unconditionally for source_numel >= 1, so source stays in the backward graph and tensor_gradient(source) returns zeros(source.shape) instead of None. Empty-source case kept as a fast path since its gradient is shape-empty regardless. Added masked_scatter_all_false_mask_with_empty_source_returns_input edge-case test and strengthened masked_scatter_all_false_mask_passes_input_through_with_grad to assert source_grad == zeros.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-c7c","title":"Add end-to-end training example and integration test","description":"FrankenTorch has e2e training loop tests buried in ft-optim's test suite, but there is no standalone, documented example showing users how to train a model. This is critical for proving the system works end-to-end and for user onboarding.

Create a comprehensive integration test (in ft-conformance or a new examples/ directory) that:

1. Defines a 2-layer MLP: Linear(784, 128) -> ReLU -> Linear(128, 10)
2. Generates synthetic MNIST-like data using the existing TensorDataset/DataLoader
3. Trains for 5 epochs using Adam optimizer with learning rate scheduling (StepLR or CosineAnnealing)
4. Tracks loss per epoch and asserts it decreases monotonically (with tolerance for noise)
5. Uses no_grad context for evaluation pass
6. Saves model state_dict to file, loads it back, verifies predictions match
7. Uses BatchNorm or LayerNorm in the architecture to exercise normalization paths
8. Logs: epoch number, batch count, loss per epoch, final accuracy estimate, elapsed time

This test exercises the full stack: ft-data (DataLoader) -> ft-nn (modules) -> ft-api (forward/backward) -> ft-autograd (gradient computation) -> ft-optim (parameter update) -> ft-serialize (save/load).

If any component fails to compose correctly, this test catches it.

Tests:
- The example IS the test: it should be a #[test] fn in ft-conformance or similar
- Assert loss decreases over 5 epochs
- Assert save/load round-trip produces identical predictions
- Assert no_grad eval context doesn't corrupt gradient state
- Log detailed per-epoch metrics for debugging","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-07T04:17:01.270413750Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T20:14:50.299054985Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T20:14:50.298796636Z","close_reason":"Added 5 E2E integration tests exercising the full stack: MLP regression with DataLoader/Adam/StepLR, binary classification with cross-entropy, save/load round-trip verification, no_grad eval safety, dataloader epoch shuffle. All pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
